description: lodash versions prior to 4.17.21 are vulnerable to Command Injection via the template function. An attacker can craft a malicious sourceURL option that, when combined with user-controlled input, executes arbitrary commands via shell interpretation.

Versions of lodash prior to 4.17.12 are vulnerable to Prototype Pollution. The function `defaultsDeep` could be tricked into adding or modifying properties of Object.prototype using a constructor payload.

PyYAML's `yaml.load()` function with the default Loader is vulnerable to arbitrary code execution when processing untrusted YAML files. An attacker can craft a YAML payload that deserializes into Python objects with side effects.

Log4Shell. Log4j2 JNDI features used in configuration, log messages, and parameters do not protect against attacker-controlled LDAP and other JNDI related endpoints. An attacker who can control log messages can execute arbitrary code loaded from LDAP servers.

summary: Authorization header leak on cross-protocol redirects
description: request will follow redirects by default. If a redirect crosses protocols (HTTP <-> HTTPS), the Authorization header is forwarded to the new host, leaking credentials. Affects request <=2.88.0.

When left-pad is called with a length parameter larger than 2^30 the function returns a very large string, potentially exhausting memory. Triggers only on attacker-controlled length input which is rarely user-influenced.
